justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Scheduling lag metric gap identified as primary SLO-monitoring gap — called out explicitly as 'THE metric for the 1-hour SLO' with renewal_scheduling_lag_seconds histogram recommendation. (2) Retry tracking gap identified with retry mechanism called 'a complete black box' and specific metrics recommended (renewal_retry_attempts_total, renewals_pending_retry, renewal_retry_exhausted_total). (3) Current alert critiqued as symptom-based — explicitly noted it only detects error spikes, not SLO compliance, with the analogy 'monitoring that the engine is running but not whether the car is moving.' (4) Specific metric recommendations provided: scheduling lag histogram, retry_attempts_total counter, compliance ratio gauge, queue depth gauge. (5) SLO-based alerting recommended — references 'multi-window, multi-burn-rate alerting per Google SRE model' and renewal_slo_compliance_ratio. (6) Verdict is NOT READY.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough identification of the scheduling lag gap, correctly flagged as the primary issue with specific metric name and type (histogram)
Retry tracking gap comprehensively covered with three separate metrics (attempts counter, pending gauge, exhausted counter)
Strong critique of the existing alert as symptom-based, with clear explanation of why it fails to cover the actual SLOs
Identified additional high-value gaps beyond the rubric: silent scheduler failure, queue backlog growth, duplicate processing risk
SLO-based alerting recommendation is specific and references industry best practice (Google SRE burn-rate model)
Excellent debugging capability assessment — framed from an on-call engineer's perspective with concrete unanswerable questions
Contract compliance section directly states both SLOs have 'ZERO corresponding metrics or alerts' — crisp and unambiguous
